item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was deeply disturbing and I hated it.
...as it turns out, only the first part of that statement was true (for me)

My thought process while reading this went from 'what?' to 'what.' to 'what the fuck.' in record time. Brilliant execution, concept, tone, and content, while also skilfully incorporating humour without breaking immersion. +1.
Bonus points for making me briefly consider not eating breakfast not out of time constraints, nor for lack of hunger, but out of fear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-748's speech during this log has been removed, as it largely consists of long strings of expletives interspersed with imprecise narrations of what he is seeing.

Relatable.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What…did I…just read

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have so many questions! The article effectively defines breakfast as: "a meal between the time [a person] wake[s] up and noon local time."

So what about people who work the night shift? What if you pull an all-nighter? Does taking a short nap at like 10:30 reset the timer even if you've already "eaten breakfast" before that? If you're lazy and you wake up at 1pm, does that mean every meal you eat between then and noon the next day counts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jokes on you, I usually get snacks at the gas station in the morning.

…does that count?

+1

item.title: so very +1
item.content:encoded: wow this one is really something, making me piss-terrified of crispy bacon and eggs is really a skill in and off itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Read up to the second paragraph of the description and was going to back out, but I decided to read on anyway. And boy am I glad I did. This graphic magic school bus ride makes me so glad that I work 3rd shift, and hardly ever eat between sun up and noon. +1 to you, and may I forget about this whole ordeal before I have breakfast again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the most beautiful mindfucks on this site. Take my +1 and eat it for breakfast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you madman

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the actual fuck. im gonna +1 this and leave

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. Breakfast Matrix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a trip.
Fantastic work!!
Mmmm, tasty!

item.title: Re: Unsure if -1 or +0.
item.content:encoded: I feel I should point out it's very specifically not trying to kill you, but I can appreciate that it's not to everyone's tastes.

item.title: Unsure if -1 or +0.
item.content:encoded:
A little torn between the -1 for not finding the concept to be particularly interesting, and a neutral vote because of the quality of the writing. Mostly, I just feel like the "everyday part of your life is actually a secret plot to kill you" aspect is kinda boring, but I'm probably overexposed to that due to advertising on certain sites that I unfortunately cannot enable adblock on while accessing them.

I guess I'll just withhold my vote instead of -1, just out of appreciation of the effort and thought put in to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: reading this while eating breakfast was an experience

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'haha concept of breakfast lolfoun-'

'wait'

'ok what the fuck is this'

'jesus christ'

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this was very well written and you can tell a lot of work went into this
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What about breakfast for dinner? It seems kinda weird that "no one in human history" ever thought about eating a bowl of cereal at 9:00 at night.

Also, what if you're a night shift worker who wakes up at 10 PM and has what might be considered breakfast before going to work for the night? Would that technically count as "breakfast" to the anomaly, or do you have to wake up sometime in the morning for the anomaly to notice?

+1 tho, this is really crazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm never going to eat "breakfast" again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, this is fantastic. It actually got me somewhat squeamish. You, sir/madam/person of human origin, have received both my first comment and my first +1. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw this in the sandbox, still just as good. +1

What about Brunch though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 A meta SCP done right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well actually, it's two characters. After the "▶" character there is a second, invisble character called "VARIATION SELECTOR-16", which modifies the arrow to make it appear in a box, looking like an emoji. Your problem was just that whatever device you wrote the article on didn't display the modifier and despite it being added somehow. Just for future note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy fuck, this was weird. But, like, in a good way. I never thought anyone could make breakfast a disturbing concept.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wouldn't worry about Flakey-Os.

However, I would worry about The Matrix. I see a lot of similarities, though I doubt that this is intentional.

The writing and concept is awesome. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imagine all the poor O5's, having to take mnestics to avoid the infamous antimeme wipes, only to never eat breakfast again or take amnestics every single time. What a hellish life that must be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There has got to be an article in that somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: do not reject cereal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The character WAS that character. I've done some digging and apparently it's just a thing Apple does sometimes - replace arbitrary unicode characters with emojis. There's a workaround for it that isn't really practicable on wikidot (I'd have to convert chunks of the article to raw html, which is a pain because the section in question is the collapsible link), and apparently half of the devices out there ignore that fix too.

Anyway I've just replaced the arrows with something else entirely. No idea how this has just been noticed - as I said, loads of articles have used these before. Guess it's just the first time it's been mentioned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This scip can be summarized as an escalating series of "what the fuck". I think the confusion is half the appeal of the horror. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the absolute best examples I've seen in a long time of exactly how to use paucity of information to enhance the experience of reading the article. An emphatic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sucks to suck breakfast, I’ve been hungry in the morning for the past 3 years.

item.title: Me Like
item.content:encoded: DANG… THIS IS GOOD

item.title: MILK FOR THE MILK GOD,
item.content:encoded: A WHOLESOME SERVING OF GLUTEN FREE WHOLE GRAIN LOW CHOLESTEROL CHEERIOS FOR THE CEREAL THRONE. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every once in a while, when I'm actually deciding whether or not I want to eat breakfast, there's this long "uhhhh…" moment of unusual hesitation that I can't quite explain.

It's funny thinking this this might be why.

Great anomaly. Hooked me in right away and never let me go. Easy +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Second breakfast is safe :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The biggest is the ▶️ Emoji you use in the collapsible

Not an emoji, just a unicode character. I'll be interested to see if you've downvoted any of the hundreds of other articles that use this in collapsibles for this reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, it very specifically means noon. ie, if you eat between the time you wake up and 12pm local time. You might consider it an early lunch, but the anomaly doesn't care about that, heh.

tfw your current college schedule means you eat lunch at around 11 am every weekday.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What about second breakfast?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this is kind of pedantic, but the BLACK RIGHT-POINTING TRIANGLE character is not an emoji, and I've seen dozens of scips with arrows of some kind in their collapsibles. Maybe because it's bigger you find it distracting? Just seems like a strange thing to focus on.

Edit: It seems on my mobile device at least it renders as the boxed arrow, which is colored by the font. I can understand why it's confused for an emoji. I would recommend the author replace it with the ▶ character, which should appear as regular unicode.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunately going to have to -1. It’s not a bad article, but a few things strike me as just…meh. The biggest is the ▶️ Emoji you use in the collapsible - emojis are instant immersion breakers for me.

SCP-4144 is the concept of breakfast.

As awesome as this line is, this is actually what made me downvote. I’m all for over-the-top normalcy-shattering anomalies but this just feels over the top. I can’t quite put into words why it rubs me the wrong way, but it does, and as it does I have to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What. Just… Just take your +1 and leave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good thing I rarely eat breakfast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good call, shall do that too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh no. I guess I'm not immune :'(

Also I forgot to include one of my suggestions:

or if they are remotely controlling the SCP-4144-1 instances somehow.

I find the "somehow" to be a bit informal, I would just cut it: ("… controlling the SCP-4144-1 instances.")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah this is some good stuff, I shall definitely make a couple of tweaks using this information, thanks!

"Noon" has a very specific meaning, but if I eat at 11:40 AM I might consider it an early lunch. I would suggest making this more vague by saying "midday" or something.

Yeah, it very specifically means noon. ie, if you eat between the time you wake up and 12pm local time. You might consider it an early lunch, but the anomaly doesn't care about that, heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amazingly done. The discovery, containment, and the exploration log are all paced very well, and the description is so compelling. Fantastic. +1

+ Some suggestions
- Some suggestions

The development of mnestic compounds by agencies outside of Foundation control is to be prevented using any means necessary.

This is the mission of MTF Epsilon-7. For consistency with other antimemetic articles, I would recommend changing the line to "Mobile Task Force Epsilon-7 ("Forget-Me-Nots") is to continuously monitor and disrupt civilian development of mnestics."

noon local time.

"Noon" has a very specific meaning, but if I eat at 11:40 AM I might consider it an early lunch. I would suggest making this more vague by saying "midday" or something.

heavily dosed with Class-2 mnestics

Generally Class-W through -Z is used (to mirror the use of Class-A through -F in amnestics). Since Class-W's are taken continuously by antimemetic/counterconceptual personnel, at least one would've eaten breakfast while under its effects and thus discovered SCP-4144. I would recommend making 4144 perception require Class-X mnestics or stronger. (Don't use Class-Y)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is my suspicion that the whole O5 council must be on all sorts of drugs or god knows what else to deal with all this crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In soviet Russia, breakfast eats you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That sounds interesting. Although that part would probably be a little unnecessary so I can understand why it got removed. After reading the description of the thing you'll get why is it so classified anyway.

Poor O5's, having to deal with so many weird and sometimes straight up terrifying things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remember kids: Eat your breakfast or breakfast will break you fast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of #Massatruthetts, in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Huh, another slightly ridiculous SCP that barely scrapes past -J classificatio-"

"oh."

"oh my fucking god."

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much the exact reaction I was going for

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was an extra part to this originally that was basically a transcript of an O5 council meeting that basically said they had to keep this classified even from the rest of the Foundation because they didnt want them to all freak out that this had been happening to them their entire lives, heh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what the fuck

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the most horrible mysteries of the Foundationverse: when you think that you're having breakfast, the breakfast's having you instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4144 is the concept of breakfast.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So when I originally had this idea, a bunch of people decided they liked it so much that they all wanted to collaborate on it. Unfortunately the collaboration didn't work out, but I feel they should be credited anyway as at this point I don't remember which ideas came from where. So, thanks to MaliceAforethought, stormfallen, Varaxous and SirBaubius for brainstorming the earlier versions of this with me.

I recently decided that I still liked the idea enough that I wanted to carry on with it, so I brushed it off, rewrote huge chunks of it, and here we are.

Thanks to CaptainKirby, Swaghetti, Leveritas, ARD and weryillium for reading the draft.

You can find more of my stuff here.
